title: Transitioning to monday.com for Social Media Automation – Possible for Native Mobile Posting?
excerpt: Hi monday.com community,I’ve been happily using ZennoPoster + ZennoDroid for over a year to automate social media tasks—full Android emulation for native Instagram/TikTok posting, scrolling, liking, and organic interacti…

posts.author: AlexKarra (AlexKarra)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi monday.com community,I’ve been happily using ZennoPoster + ZennoDroid for over a year to automate social media tasks—full Android emulation for native Instagram/TikTok posting, scrolling, liking, and organic interactions. It delivers real mobile feeds and keeps bans low, which has been great for scaling.But after seeing monday.com’s boards, automations, and apps like Publisher/Social Media Manager (especially the one-click publishing to Instagram, TikTok, etc.), I’m seriously considering switching some workflows here. The visual planning, calendars, approvals, and integrations look clean and team-friendly—no more heavy scripting.Quick question: Does monday.com (or its apps) support anything like native mobile emulation for posting/interacting in Instagram/TikTok apps? Or is it mostly web/API-based scheduling? Browser views often show different content than real phones (limited Reels, no native gestures), so I’m curious about workarounds or upcoming features for true mobile behavior.monday.com seems perfect for managing campaigns end-to-end—thinking of moving here for easier collaboration and less custom code. Any tips from folks who’ve done similar transitions?Thanks!

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @AlexKarra
monday.com is great for planning, approvals, calendars, and automations, but it doesn’t support native mobile emulation or “in-app” Instagram/TikTok interactions like scrolling, liking, or gesture-based behavior.

Most social publishing in monday.com (and apps) is web/API-based scheduling and posting. A common setup is using monday.com as the workflow hub, and keeping a dedicated mobile/emulation tool for anything that needs true phone behavior.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:
